title: Kansas City Police Launch Real-Time 911 Translation for World Cup Visitors
detail: Kansas City Police Department activated a real-time 911 emergency translation service enabling dispatchers to communicate in multiple languages with non-English-speaking World Cup 2026 visitors. The service, part of Kansas City's host-city preparedness initiative, ensures that international fans attending matches at Arrowhead Stadium can access emergency services in their native language — a practical step ahead of an expected influx of fans from over 100 countries.

media.caption: Kansas City Police Department activates multilingual 911 service for World Cup 2026

title: FIFA World Cup Trophy Tour Arrives in Winnipeg
detail: The FIFA World Cup Trophy Tour by Coca-Cola arrived in Winnipeg, Manitoba — the third Canadian city on the seven-city tour following Vancouver (April 9–11) and Calgary (April 12). The original Jules Rimet-era World Cup Trophy was on display for Manitoba residents as part of FIFA's 'Canada Celebrates' fan engagement programme, which brings a World Cup stop within a two-hour drive of 75% of Canadian residents in 34 communities across the country.

media.caption: FIFA World Cup Trophy Tour 'Canada Celebrates' visits Winnipeg — third Canadian city stop
